channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities in Asia and around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Lunar New Year festivals and prayers marked the start of the Year of the Snake around Asia and farther afield on Wednesday — including in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Several people are feared dead in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Hours after a massive rush of Hindu pilgrims set off a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival in northern India early Wednesday, Indian authorities were yet to release casualty figures even as the country's prime minister acknowledged the loss of lives and local media reports sa
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 Palestinians killed by Israeli fire in occupied West Bank, officials say
item.description: The Palestinian Health Ministry says two Palestinians were killed by Israeli fire in the occupied West Bank overnight and into Wednesday. A 23-year-old man was shot dead in Tulkarem and a 25-year-old man was killed in a strike on Jenin, where Israel launched a large operation earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl brings Arab and Israeli scientists together
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — It soars over the borders and divisions of the war-torn Middle East, bringing Arab and Israeli academics together. An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl is now getting global attention.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims is in legal limbo as an unusual trial starts
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — When Hawaii Gov. Josh Green announced a $4 billion settlement about a year after the deadliest U.S. wildfire in a century devastated Lahaina in 2023, he touted the speed of the deal to “avoid protracted and painful lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: New results from a national exam find that America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, ending her 11-year run with Phoenix
item.description: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, marking the end of her 11-year career with the Phoenix Mercury.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas Gov. Abbott orders DPS tactical strike teams to assist federal illegal immigration enforcement efforts
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott ordered DPS tactical strike teams to assist the Trump administration's efforts to go after illegal immigrants inside the state, as Austin law enforcement officials vow to not comply.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson And Claire Savage, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Head Start and Medicaid providers hit glitches as Trump freezes federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump paused federal grants and loans in a far-reaching directive, early childhood education centers and states discovered Tuesday they could no longer access money they rely on to provide care for some of the nation's neediest families and children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sisters convicted in planning murder-for-hire scheme
item.description: FAIRHOPE, Ala. (WKRG) — Two Fairhope sisters were convicted on multiple charges in a murder-for-hire scheme, according to the United States Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Pat O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Carnivore diet sends man to hospital with hands filled with cholesterol
item.description: The patient was eventually diagnosed with a condition known as xanthelasma — the build-up of cholesterol under the skin.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: (The Hill) -- One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Study says climate change made conditions that fed California wildfires more likely, more intense
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Human-caused climate change increased the likelihood and intensity of the hot, dry and windy conditions that fanned the flames of the recent devastating Southern California wildfires, a scientific study found.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illegal immigrant suspect in Rachel Morin's murder expected to argue for a change of venue
item.description: An illegal immigrant from El Salvador is expected to seek a change of venue out of Harford County, Maryland, where he is accused of killing Rachel Morin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sean Duffy is confirmed by the Senate to lead the Transportation Department
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Duffy was confirmed Tuesday as transportation secretary, giving him a key role in helping President Donald Trump cut regulations and fix the nation's infrastructure. The former Wisconsin congressman has promised safer Boeing planes, less regulation and help for U.S.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Workers save kidnapped girl at Dothan hotel, police say
item.description: A Florida man is behind bars after police say he sexually assaulted and kidnapped a girl at an Alabama hotel.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: "We are devastated by the accident involving a team member at Charlotte International Airport (CLT)," a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola introduces new flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup. Coca-Cola Orange Cream is scheduled to go on sale Feb. 10 in the U.S. and Canada. It will be sold in regular and zero-sugar varieties. Atlanta-based Coca-Cola Co.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House press briefing discusses federal assistance, deportation
item.description: Karoline Leavitt addressed the media from the White House's briefing room for the first time on Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Proposal would require parents to provide immigration status of students
item.description: “OKCPS does not, nor do we have plans to, collect the immigration status of our students or their families,” she said.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, WGN 9

item.title: Apple software update will activate AI by default, but there is a way to turn it off
item.description: iPhone users could have a somewhat surprising new feature on their devices after their next software update.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First US case of H5N9 bird flu strain found in California
item.description: The first confirmed case of H5N9 bird flu in the United States has been identified in Merced County, California, and control measures are being undertaken to contain the virus.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, WGN 9

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WGN 9

item.title: Jets agree to terms with Broncos executive Darren Mougey to be their general manager
item.description: The New York Jets' lengthy searches are over, and they're pairing a first-time general manager with a first-time head coach to try to turn things around.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK JR., Associated Press, Fox 5
